findings.message: Hardcoded secret committed in source code. The `api_secret_key` field has a default value of "super-secret-key-do-not-share" embedded directly in the codebase. Any secret committed to a repository must be considered compromised: it is exposed to anyone with repo access, leaks via git history forever, and bypasses environment-based secret management. This is an OWASP Top 10 A02 (Cryptographic Failures) / A05 (Security Misconfiguration) issue and CWE-798 (Use of Hard-coded Credentials). It also contradicts the class docstring's own rule: "All configuration, sourced from environment / .env. Never hardcode config."
findings.suggestion: Remove the default and require the value to come from the environment (APP_API_SECRET_KEY via pydantic-settings). Use `api_secret_key: SecretStr` (with no default, or `Field(...)` to mark it required) so the app fails fast at startup if unset. Also rotate the leaked value and purge it from git history.
